zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas de Ashton於1440年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾艾希頓 武安德 林時，他的父親Sir John Ashton17歲，母親Dulcia Trafford23歲。他於1493年之後與Agnes Harrington在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1514年7月21日在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ashton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called, especially Ashton-under-Lyne near Manchester. Most are named from Old English æsc ‘ash tree’ + tūn ‘settlement’; the one in Northamptonshire is (æt thǣm) æscum ‘(at the) ash trees’. Others have been assimilated to this from different sources. The one in Devon is ‘the settlement (tūn) of Æschere’, while the one in Hertfordshire is ‘the settlement of Ælli’.

variants.name: Aspden
meanings: English: habitational name from Aspden (alias Aspen) in Oswaldtwistle, Lancashire. This surname has been confused with   Ashton   and   Austin  , and probably also with   Aston   and   Astin  , all of which are common surnames in Lancashire.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas de Ashton was born in 1440, in Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Ashton, was 17 and his mother, Dulcia Trafford, was 23. He married Agnes Harrington after 1493, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 21 July 1514, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, at the age of 74.

Il a épousé Agnes Harrington après 1493, à Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 21 juillet 1514, à Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

ko.Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 애슈턴 운더 리니
